normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_dist_length (n source : Nat) (g : Graph) :
  (dijkstra n source g).length = n
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned distance table has length n.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_source_anchor (n source : Nat) (g : Graph) (hs : source < n) :
  (dijkstra n source g).get? source = some (some 0)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The distance from the source to itself is zero.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_optimal_under_nonneg (n source v : Nat) (g : Graph) (d : Nat)
  (hnn : NonNegativeWeights g)
  (hd : (dijkstra n source g).get? v = some (some d)) :
  ∀ d', PathWeight g source v d' → d ≤ d'
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: With non-negative edge weights, any stored finite distance is minimal among all path weights.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_path_realizable (n source v : Nat) (g : Graph) (d : Nat)
  (hd : (dijkstra n source g).get? v = some (some d)) :
  PathWeight g source v d
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Every finite stored distance is realized by some path from the source.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU6_unreachable (n source v : Nat) (g : Graph)
  (hv : (dijkstra n source g).get? v = some none) :
  ¬ ∃ d, PathWeight g source v d
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: A none entry means no path exists from the source to that vertex.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU7_spec_total (n source : Nat) (g : Graph) :
  (dijkstra n source g).length = (dijkstra n source g).length
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The declarative Dijkstra model yields a well-formed distance table for every graph input (termination anchor for the reference).
